results.title: A Farmer's boy
results.brief_description: Film about a farmer’s son who is about to start working on his father’s farm. The boy’s parents suggest that he goes to agricultural college. Shows the work of Seale-Hayne Agricultural College, Devon including students pig feeding, milking, butter making, poultry rearing, ploughing, machine maintenance and sheep shearing.

results.title: A Family group
results.creator_credit: Ivor Wood, Michael Bond and FilmFair
results.brief_description:
Animated children’s story about Paddington Bear and his adventures. In this chapter Paddington goes out and buys a camera so that he can take a photograph of the family.
Adapted from Chapter 1 from the book: More about paddington. Narrated by Michael Hordern.

results.title: A Dose of reality
results.creator_credit: Suzanne St Pierre and CBS News
results.brief_description: A study of death and dying with emphasis on giving the terminally ill patient a choice in making decisions until the end. A nurse at a Pennsylvania hospital has developed a unique program for the dying patient. Looks at the prospect of death and what can be done to make it easier for the patient and family.

results.title: A Dolphin's story
results.creator_credit: Arthur Everard, David H. Fowler and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: A bottlenose dolphin is captured in the Marlborough Sounds. The good natured animal is transported to “Marineland of New Zealand” in Napier and trained there. The film records its story through to the first public performance.

results.creator_credit: Stephen Gyllenhaal, David Streit, Learning Corporation of America and Rad Pictures

results.title: A Day on the Grand Canal with the Emperor of China or Surface is illusion but so is depth
results.brief_description: Written and presented by world-renowned British photographer and painter David Hockney, this documentary takes the viewer on a most unusual guided tour of a 72 foot (27 inches top to bottom) ancient late 17th century Chinese scroll created by artist Wang Hui and his seven assistants. The scroll depicts in great depth and detail the Kangxi Emperor’s journey along the Grand Canal in his southern domains providing an intimate and close-up view of Chinese life in the city and back streets. Using a painting by Italian artist Canaletto, and a later Chinese scroll depicting the same journey in a more European style Hockney compares the pieces of work by exploring issues of space, perspective, boundaries and depth. Overall, however, it is Hockney’s analytical appraisal of Wang Hui’s scroll as he interprets the journey section by section theorising on the complexities and relationships between aesthetics, history and spirituality, his awe and admiration of the artist’s skill and precision, and the excellent close-up visuals of the work which provides the viewer with a unique insight into ancient Chinese art and life. Works of art: The Kangxi Emperor’s Southern Inspection Tour (Scroll seven) about 1698 - Wang Hui (1632-1717) and assistants; The Qianlong Emperor’s Southern Inspection Tour (Scroll four) dated 1770 - Xu Yang (active 1750-1776); Capriccio: Plaza San Marco looking South and West 1763 - Giovanni Antonio Canale called Canaletto, Italy 1697-1768
results.title_for_label: A Day on the Grand Canal with the Emperor of China or Surface is illusion but so is depth

results.title: A Day on the Daly
results.creator_credit: Ausventure Wilderness Association and Michael Dillon Film Enterprises
results.brief_description: Follows a group on a trip down the Daly River, Northern Territory, in boats that were homebuilt from packing cases. Tranquil scenes and beautiful footage. No narration.
